title: Gift Card Options

title: Sun Protection Systems

title: Passion on Water
description: How do individuals pursue transformative journeys on unconventional vessels as acts of personal redemption, cultural revival, or ecological innovation?
narrative: The user seeks stories of people who turned adversity or societal constraints into profound journeys using non-traditional boats—not for sport or commerce, but as symbols of resilience, sustainability, or cultural reclamation. Relevant documents include Capucine Trochet’s Atlantic crossing on Tara Tari, a jute-fiber sailboat built to revive Bangladesh’s jute industry and embody ecological ingenuity; the Ohlone community’s revival of tule-reed canoe-building to honor ancestral traditions and protest environmental injustice; and the poetic metaphor of the Espanola as a vessel of personal liberation. These reflect deep personal, cultural, or environmental motivations. Not relevant are documents about competitive rowing, model boat building, or maritime regulations, which focus on technique, law, or recreation rather than symbolic or transformative journeys.

title: Collaborative Economy Taxation

Relevant documents must detail the 2017 Finance Act provisions, thresholds for taxation (e.g., €7,720 for movable goods, €23,000 for real estate), exemptions for cost-sharing activities like carpooling, and the role of platform-generated income statements. Irrelevant documents about car rental agencies, vehicle energy labels, or general carpooling history without tax implications are not relevant.

title: Late Blight-Resistant Potatoes

Irrelevant documents discuss unrelated fungal diseases (e.g., Fusarium), organic growing methods, or general potato cultivation without addressing genetic resistance. Relevance is determined by explicit mention of resistance traits, breeding programs (e.g., INRA), field performance under blight pressure, and reduction in chemical use—not general gardening tips or unrelated crop diseases.

title: Caesar's Gaul

Relevant documents must discuss Caesar’s tactics (divide-and-rule, scorched earth), the resistance led by Vercingétorix, the fate of Gallic tribes like the Cadurques, the erasure of pre-Roman social structures, and the cultural annihilation—especially the marginalization of women’s roles and Celtic spirituality. Documents that romanticize or fictionalize Gallic culture (e.g., folk metal bands, modern lyre reconstructions) or discuss unrelated topics (e.g., Gallicanism in the Church) are not relevant, even if they mention Gaul or Caesar.
